_meta.author: Claude (Adam-supervised)
_meta.note: FXBG City Council + Commonwealth's Attorney — evidence-based finalization. Roster verified current June 2026. Scores reset to null where no evidence (prior speculative party-default FALSEs cleared); FALSE only where a documented vote/statement/endorsement exists; TRUE where documented alignment. Adds the v5.0 local categories (public_justice, refuse_state_overreach) missing from these records.

kerry-devine.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Mayor, At-Large, term 2024-2027 (fredericksburgva.gov/263). HARD evidence: voted for FY2027 budget raising the real-estate tax rate 4 cents to $0.84/$100 and a two-tier water/sewer rate hike, and personally pushed to advertise a rate 1 cent higher 'for flexibility' (economic_stewardship FALSE). Publicly supported the city Gun Give-Back and backed Gov. Northam's proposal giving localities more authority to regulate firearms (self_defense FALSE). As mayor, the city officially observes LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, flies Pride flags downtown, and maintains a Commitment to Equity & Inclusion (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE, institutional). The FY2027 budget she presided over funds a public-safety pay plan with no defunding — counted as routine governance, not the affirmative public advocacy public_justice q0 requires, so left null. No church/faith affiliation found in two long-form interviews or her official bio. NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, schools curriculum, election administration, or immigration at the council level — those cells are null, not penalized.

2.note: Voted for the FY2027 budget raising the real-estate tax rate 4 cents to $0.84/$100 plus a two-tier water/sewer rate hike; pushed to advertise an even higher rate.

4.note: As mayor, the city officially celebrates LGBTQIA+ Pride Month, flies Pride flags downtown, and maintains an equity-and-inclusion commitment; she has not opposed these.

0.note: Publicly supported the city Gun Give-Back program and backed Gov. Northam's proposal to give localities more authority to regulate firearms.

profile.religion: Reported member, Shiloh Baptist Church (Old Site), Fredericksburg (secondary source)

charlie-frye-jr.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: Vice Mayor, Ward 4, re-elected/named VM Jan 2026, term 2026-2029. HARD evidence: founded the Fredericksburg Gun Give-Back in 2014 (242 firearms collected by Jan 2026), championed statewide HB702 modeled on it, and backed expanding local firearm-regulation authority (self_defense FALSE). Voted for two consecutive 4-cent real-estate tax-rate increases — FY2026 to $0.80 and FY2027 to $0.84/$100 (economic_stewardship FALSE). Public safety his stated No. 1 priority; runs police-partnership programs ('Murder Free Fredericksburg') and credited the city with zero homicides in 2025 (public_justice TRUE). Prior speculative party-default FALSEs on abortion/marriage/elections cleared to null — NO direct evidence of personal positions on those was found.

2.note: Voted for two consecutive 4-cent real-estate tax-rate increases (FY2026 to $0.80, FY2027 to $0.84/$100).

0.note: Public safety his stated No. 1 priority; runs police-partnership programs (Gun Give-Back, 'Murder Free Fredericksburg'); credited zero homicides in 2025.
Founded the Fredericksburg Gun Give-Back (242 firearms by Jan 2026); championed statewide HB702; backed expanding local firearm-regulation authority.

1.note: Championed gun give-back expansion via HB702.

profile.religion: St. George's Episcopal Church, Fredericksburg (vestry member)
profile.background: Licensed Clinical Social Worker (private practice since 1998); former Fredericksburg School Board member 2010-2023 (Chair/Vice Chair). At-Large council member, term 2024-2027.

jannan-holmes.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large, term 2024-2027; ran as Democratic-endorsed candidate (fredericksburgdems.org). HARD evidence (primary-source council minutes): voted aye on FY2026 and FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increases, water/sewer rate hikes, and the annual budgets (economic_stewardship FALSE). She voted to appropriate a citizen donation to the Fredericksburg Police Department and the FY2027 budget directs ~$36.4M to public safety, but these are routine budget/appropriation actions rather than the affirmative public backing of law enforcement that public_justice q0 requires, so that cell is left null. LCSW; St. George's Episcopal (vestry). NO EVIDENCE found on life/marriage/family/elections/immigration/2A at the council level — those cells null.

2.note: Voted aye on FY2026/FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increases, water/sewer rate hikes, and the annual budgets.

profile.background: Associate Professor of History, University of Mary Washington (PhD, Michigan). Chaired the city Economic Development Authority before election. At-Large council member, term 2024-2027.

will-mackintosh.notes_append: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster verified: At-Large, term 2024-2027. HARD evidence: endorsed by the LGBTQ+ Victory Fund (verified via Victory Fund's own site, 2023); openly gay, married to husband Brian; publicly praised downtown Pride flag displays (biblical_marriage q0/q2/q4 FALSE). Authored/defended the FY2026 4-cent tax increase ('a conservative budget in the old-school, small-C sense') and voted for the FY2027 4-cent increase and two-tier water/sewer hike (economic_stewardship FALSE). Personally proposed exploring Ranked-Choice Voting for city elections (election_integrity q0 FALSE — contrary to hand-counted-paper-ballot simplicity). No church/faith affiliation found. NO EVIDENCE on life/family/immigration/2A individually — null.

0.note: Endorsed by the LGBTQ+ Victory Fund (verified, 2023); openly gay, married to a husband — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

2.note: Supportive of LGBTQ-affirming city posture; Victory Fund-endorsed.

4.note: Publicly praised downtown Pride flag displays as a 'testament to the vibrancy of downtown.'

2.note: Authored/defended the FY2026 4-cent real-estate tax increase; voted for the FY2027 increase and two-tier water/sewer hike.

0.note: Personally proposed exploring Ranked-Choice Voting for future city elections (contrary to hand-counted-paper-ballot simplicity).

profile.background: GIS Administrator, Stafford County (GISP); Hampden-Sydney College. Former Fredericksburg City School Board chair (Jan 2025) and former Bowling Green Town councilman; 2016 Democratic nominee for VA-01. Ward 1 council member, term 2026-2029.

matt-rowe-fxbg.notes_set: Ward 1 council member (term 2026-2029); Democratic-endorsed (Fredericksburg Democratic Committee, Nov 2025). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: voted YES on the FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increase to $0.84/$100 and the overall budget (economic_stewardship FALSE). Notably, he was the LONE 6-1 dissenter against the FY2027 two-tier water/sewer rate structure — but his stated reason was renter/affordability EQUITY (apartment residents paying more per gallon), not tax restraint, and he still supported the headline tax increase. As school-board chair he authored a professional-development spending-control policy (fiscal-governance positive, school-board tier). 2016 (VA-01 race): supported comprehensive immigration reform, legal status for undocumented workers, and protecting DREAMers (documented but a federal-race position; no local ICE vote, so border cells left null). NO EVIDENCE on life/marriage/family/Christian-liberty/elections at the council level — those cells null.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increase and budget; was the lone dissenter on the utility rate structure but on renter-equity grounds, not tax restraint.

profile.background: Chef/restaurateur; co-founded FOODE (2011) and owns Mercantile and the 718 event venue in downtown Fredericksburg; Bravo 'Top Chef' competitor. First Black woman on Fredericksburg City Council; part of the first female-majority council (Nov 2025). Ward 2, term 2026-2029. Active with Micah Ecumenical Ministries.

joy-crump-fxbg.notes_set: Ward 2 council member (term 2026-2029); Democratic-endorsed (Fredericksburg Democratic Committee). Took office 2026 — short voting record. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: voted for the FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increase to $0.84/$100 and the two-tier water/sewer rate hike (economic_stewardship FALSE). Community ties to local churches (St. George's Episcopal, First Baptist, Micah Ecumenical Ministries) reported as outreach work, not a confirmed personal membership. Prior speculative party-default FALSEs cleared to null; NO EVIDENCE found on life/marriage/family/elections/justice/immigration/2A — those cells null pending an accumulating record.

2.note: Voted for the FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increase and the two-tier water/sewer rate hike (May 2026).

profile.background: Urban planner (AICP); manages long-range planning for the U.S. Navy. BA Historic Preservation (UMW), MURP (VCU). Former chair, Fredericksburg Planning Commission. Appointed Feb 2025 to fill a vacancy; elected Nov 2025. Ward 3, term 2026-2029. Self-identifies as Independent (declined party endorsement, citing the Hatch Act) but ran on the Democratic-endorsed slate.

susanna-finn.notes_set: Ward 3 council member (term 2026-2029); self-identifies as Independent (declined party endorsement under the Hatch Act as a federal employee) but ran on the Democratic-endorsed slate. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: voted YES on the FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increase, the $141.5M budget, the two-tier water/sewer hike, and release of a $6M holdback; proposed new sustainability + affordable-housing trust funds (economic_stewardship FALSE). Was the sole NO vote on a police-requested automated red-light-camera program — a civil-liberties/anti-surveillance posture that does NOT indicate defunding, so public_justice left null. No church/faith affiliation found. NO EVIDENCE on life/marriage/family/elections/immigration/2A individually — null.

2.note: Voted YES on the FY2027 4-cent real-estate tax increase, the $141.5M budget, the two-tier water/sewer hike, and a $6M holdback release; proposed new sustainability and affordable-housing trust funds.

profile.background: Commonwealth's Attorney for the City of Fredericksburg since Aug 2021 (re-elected Nov 2025, 66%-32%). Career prosecutor 21+ yrs (Charlottesville CA 1999-2018; Fredericksburg Deputy CA 2018-2021). JD University of Virginia, BA Dartmouth.

libby-humphries.notes_set: Commonwealth's Attorney, City of Fredericksburg (elected chief prosecutor since 2021; re-elected Nov 2025). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence — self-described reform/progressive prosecutor: runs an explicit cashless (risk-based) bail policy (public_justice q1 FALSE); frames criminal behavior 'from a public health standpoint' and prioritizes 'community safety and health over punishment' with expanded diversion/alternatives to incarceration (public_justice q3, q4 FALSE); supports Fair and Just Prosecution (a national progressive-prosecutor network) and the PD's '8 Can't Wait' reforms; named 'the progressive choice' by the Progressive Voters Guide in 2021 and 2025. Counter-evidence (mixed, so q2 left null not FALSE): she retains victim-services staff and has a real violent-crime conviction record, including six life terms against a child abuser and multiple murder convictions. NO blanket declination-to-prosecute policy found. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/Christian-liberty/elections/state-overreach/immigration — null.

1.note: Explicit cashless bail: 'An exclusively risk-based bail policy is a cashless bail policy'; supports Fair and Just Prosecution (progressive-prosecutor network).

3.note: Frames criminal behavior 'from a public health standpoint'; prioritizes 'community safety and health over punishment.'

4.note: Reform prosecutor focused on decarceration and alternatives to incarceration; does not affirm a retributive 'bear the sword' posture.
